PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: a star out of reach
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: Home is a star I can no longer point to. Everyone I left is decades of silence away, and no message of mine will outrun the dark.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: a letter that cannot arrive in time
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: I sent a letter home the year I left. If it ever arrives, the woman who reads it will have my mother's name and no memory of me.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: one long cold blink
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: I crossed between worlds in cryptosleep once. One long cold blink, and every debt, every friend, every enemy I had was a century stale.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: counting the years awake
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: They warn you about waking from cryptosleep: count your fingers, then count the years. The fingers are always all there.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: a story for children
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: They say on the glitterworlds no one dies unless they tire of living, and medicine mends anything. I used to think it was a story for children.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: where sickness was a clerical error
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: I grew up where sickness was a clerical error and the streets cleaned themselves. Every scar I have taken since feels like a mistake in the paperwork.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: a world of plow and prayer
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: My world plowed, prayed, and buried by the seasons. A machine that talked would have been burned as a demon, and I would have carried the torch.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: towns like these
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: I grew up in towns not so different from this: dirt roads, radios, rumors of the stars. The rim never felt strange to me, only farther.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: gods of metal beneath the world
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: The elders said gods of metal think beneath the world, dreaming thoughts a lifetime wide. We left offerings at the ruins and never dug there.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: living inside their weather
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: I read that archotechs think thoughts a thousand years wide, and that we live inside their weather without ever seeing the storm.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: weather made of minds
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: Where I come from, minds had weather. Some days a hum from nowhere made the whole camp weep or sing, and you waited it out like rain.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: when the link first opened
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: When the link first opened in my skull, silence grew a texture. My first psychic storm I remember the way sailors remember first going under.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: old machines under the mountains
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: The elders said the old machines sleep under the mountains, and that waking them once cost the ancestors their whole world.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: a war gone quiet, not ended
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: The war against the machines never ended anywhere. It only goes quiet for a generation, and then someone's scanner finds something warm.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: steel taught to listen
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: The first time a mech answered my thought, I understood the old war differently: somebody, long ago, taught the steel to listen.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: what the books never mention
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: The books said insectoids were bred as living weapons for tunnels no mech could hold. The books never mention the sound they make.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: the miners' one rule
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: Every miner I ever drank with had the same rule: when the drill breaks into a warm hollow, you run before you look.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: built for war
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: My line was built for war: steady hands, quiet blood, orders that itch until obeyed. Whatever I make of myself, that came first.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: centuries like winters
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: I remember centuries the way others remember winters, and the thirst was always part of the bargain. Long life keeps its ledgers in other people.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: blood older than cities
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: My blood is older than the cities. The quick people talk past me, but the cold, hunger, and grief were bred out of them and not out of me.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: born to poisoned air
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: I was born to poisoned air, in a family that called the green worlds a fairy tale. Clean rain still feels like someone else's luck.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: ceremony is a kind of gravity
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: I grew up under the Empire's titles, where a bow measured in degrees could raise you or bury you. Ceremony is a kind of gravity.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: no fleet that polite
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: When the Empire came to our sky, they brought lances, livery, and manners. The old folk said no fleet that polite arrives without a bill.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: where signals go to die
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: A freighter man once told me there are places between the stars where signals go to die. He never said what listens there, and I never asked.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: the devil's bargain
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: I watched a man take luciferium to save his legs. It saved them. He was never sick again, and never his own again either.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: the mountain that walks
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: I saw a thrumbo once, at dawn, tall as a granary and calm as snowfall. The hunters who talk biggest went very quiet that day.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: blades that whisper
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: They tell of blades that whisper to their bearers and mourn them after. I believed it the day I saw a duelist thank his sword.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: memories arrived pre-owned
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: I was not born so much as decanted. My earliest memories arrived pre-owned, and I have never trusted the word "childhood".

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: salvage law in hull burns
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: I ran with rim fleets where salvage law was written in hull burns. Every ship I have loved was stolen at least twice.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: stars fall burning
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: By the first fire of winter, the elders told how stars fall burning and strangers climb out. Half our tools began as someone's falling star.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: a cough counted like ammunition
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: Out here a cough gets counted like ammunition. Everyone I traveled with could name the person the plague took from them.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: when the wind tastes of metal
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: I have seen a sky turn the color of old bruises and the grass die in a week. You learn young: when the wind tastes of metal, go inside.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: candles for a reason
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: The worst nights were when the sky itself failed, sun gone dark or the machines dead quiet. Old-timers kept candles for a reason.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: the day the link took
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: The day the link took, every quiet room stopped being empty. I had joined a conversation older than any city, and it had noticed me.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: a before and after in the blood
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: I went to sleep one kind of human and woke another. My own blood keeps a before and an after now, and both of them answer to my name.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: rewritten line by line
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: They rewrote me line by line, and I signed for it. Whoever wakes in my bed tomorrow will remember being me; I try to find that comforting.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: when the silence of machines ended
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: The day the mechlink settled in, the silence of machines ended for me. Now I know what the old commanders heard: everything, waiting.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: a name grown heavier
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: The day the Empire first spoke my title aloud, my name grew heavier. Some doors opened; others closed forever behind the honorific.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: rank is a debt
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: With every new title I learned the same lesson again: rank is a debt the Empire lets you carry in public, with interest due at court.

PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.label: sleep to wagon wheels
PawnDiary.DiaryLoreSeedDef.text: I learned to sleep to wagon wheels and wake at strange bells. Everything on the rim was carried there by somebody tired.
